seed_problem: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
mutation: input+theme: skin the job-shop as a traffic-signal grid where convoys are jobs, intersections are machines and green windows are operations; routes are grid walks with heavy-tailed green windows and shared intersections, so the min-makespan schedule is NP-hard and dispatch/list-scheduling/active-schedule/randomised strategies diverge. Scoring uses a checker-built fully-serial baseline B (sum of green windows) instead of an unknown optimum.
title: Green Wave: Convoy Signal Scheduling on a Traffic Grid
strategies: fully-serial baseline: run every green window back-to-back with no overlap (makespan = sum of windows)
earliest-start list scheduling: repeatedly dispatch the ready stop with the smallest earliest start (SPT tie-break)
active-schedule generation (Giffler-Thompson) with SPT/LPT/min-completion priority rules
randomised multi-restart active-schedule search: seeded random resolution of machine conflicts, keeping the lowest-makespan timetable found
